CREATE TABLE test_dsn (id INT PRIMARY KEY, dsn_name VARCHAR(255), description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_dsn (id, dsn_name, description) VALUES (1, 'TestDSN1', 'First Test');
INSERT INTO test_dsn (id, dsn_name, description) VALUES (2, 'TestDSN2', 'Second Test');
INSERT INTO test_dsn (id, dsn_name, description) VALUES (3, 'TestDSN3', 'Third Test');
SELECT dsn_name FROM test_dsn WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_dsn WHERE description LIKE 'Test%');
UPDATE test_dsn SET description = 'Updated Description' WHERE dsn_name = 'TestDSN1';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dsn WHERE dsn_name = 'TestDSN3';
SELECT dsn_name FROM test_dsn AS d INNER JOIN (SELECT id FROM test_dsn WHERE id > 1) AS sub ON d.id = sub.id;
SELECT dsn_name, description FROM test_dsn WHERE dsn_name LIKE 'Test%' ORDER BY id DESC;
SELECT dsn_name FROM test_dsn WHERE dsn_name = 'NonExistent' UNION SELECT dsn_name FROM test_dsn WHERE dsn_name = 'TestDSN1';
CREATE TABLE dsn_logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, test_id INT, status VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO dsn_logs (log_id, test_id, status) SELECT 1, id, 'created' FROM test_dsn WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM dsn_logs WHERE created_at > (SELECT MIN(created_at) FROM dsn_logs);
UPDATE dsn_logs SET status = 'updated' WHERE log_id = (SELECT MAX(log_id) FROM dsn_logs);
